title: Gas sensors and real-time video for accurate classroom occupancy detection
author: Challa, AlHmoud, et al.

steps.title: static occupancy, where students remained seated throughout the session; (

steps.title: dynamic occupancy, where students entered and...
steps.instruction: dynamic occupancy, where students entered and exited the room at irregular intervals; and (

steps.title: partial occlusion, where classroom furniture and...
steps.instruction: partial occlusion, where classroom furniture and other obstacles partially obscured the camera’s field of view. A total of 15 h of data was collected across 10 sessions, each lasting 90 min, with synchronized CO₂ sensor readings and video feeds ensuring seamless integration of multimodal data.

steps.title: Method validation and results
steps.instruction: Performance was assessed using key validation metrics: detection accuracy (the proportion of correctly identified occupants compared to manual headcounts), false positive and negative rates, environmental robustness (system reliability under varying lighting and occlusion conditions), and system latency (time elapsed between data acquisition and output generation). These metrics provide a comprehensive evaluation of the methodology’s ability to deliver accurate and timely occupancy detection under real-world classroom conditions..
